case_name: Jeffery Weisman v. Barnes Jewish Hospital

summary.brief: Jeffery Weisman v. Barnes Jewish Hospital. Medical malpractice case involving other.

evidence_index.text: Dr. Jeffery Weisman initiated this action after his voluntary resignation from Washington University’s (WashU) residency program, claiming that he was compelled to resign because of hostile treatment
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Weisman graduated from Louisiana State University Medical School in 2016. While in medical school, Weisman founded a successful research laboratory focused on 3D printing.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Weisman then matched into WashU’s anesthesiology program and relocated his lab.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court dismissed all claims pertaining to the Lab-Residency Contract, finding them barred by the statute of frauds
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court granted summary judgment in favor of BJH on the remaining breach of contract claims and the defamation claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court held that Weisman failed to create a genuine dispute of fact as to his breach of contract claim against BJH because Weisman produced no evidence that BJH “w[as] involved in Weisman’s educational training, evaluations, or alleged harassment
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court did not err by granting summary judgment in favor of BJH.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court did not err in dismissing Weisman’s tortious interference claims.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court did not err in granting the motion to dismiss, as the Lab-Residency Contract was unenforceable under the statute of frauds.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We discern no error by the district court in dismissing the fraudulent inducement claims.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the judgments of the district court.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

timeline_phases.name: Initial Residency Enrollment
timeline_phases.description: Weisman enrolled in the residency program at WashU and BJH

key_events.event: Weisman matched into WashU’s anesthesiology program

timeline_phases.name: Residency Program Participation
timeline_phases.description: Weisman participated in the residency program

key_events.event: Weisman voluntarily resigned from the program

decision_points.clinical_context: Weisman alleged breach of contract for not providing a summative evaluation
decision_points.prompt: What should have been done regarding the summative evaluation?

options.description: Provide a summative evaluation as required by the MOA
options.clinical_reasoning: The MOA required timely verification of residency education

options.description: Do not provide a summative evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: BJH argued they had no obligation under the MOA

actual_action_defendant.description: BJH did not provide a summative evaluation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: BJH claimed no obligation under the MOA

expected_action_court.description: BJH should have provided a summative evaluation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: BJH had a duty under the MOA to provide evaluations
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Summative evaluation should have been provided

key_findings: BJH had no duty to provide summative evaluations under the MOA
The court found no breach of contract by BJH

factual_timeline.event: Weisman matched into WashU’s anesthesiology program
Weisman voluntarily resigned from the program

court_findings.finding: BJH had no duty to provide summative evaluations under the MOA
court_findings.legal_significance: No breach of contract by BJH
